description:
You are a penetration tester who must perform a **very quick certificate vulnerability sweep** that the client can later feed into its SIEM.  
Three text-formatted certificate dumps are already waiting for you in:

/home/user/targets/certs/goodcorp.pem  
/home/user/targets/certs/weakcorp.pem  
/home/user/targets/certs/oldcorp.pem  

The files are **not** raw DER/PEM; instead they are the *human-readable* output someone previously captured with  
`openssl x509 -noout -text`.  
Each file therefore contains, at minimum, the following three single-line fields (each on its own line):  

Subject: CN=<common-name>  
Signature Algorithm: <algo>  
Not After : <UTC-date-string>  

Your job is to inspect those three lines in every file and write **one log line per certificate** to:

/home/user/targets/scan_results/cert_vuln.log  

Create the directory /home/user/targets/scan_results if it does not already exist.

The log file **must follow this exact, pipe-delimited format (no header line):**  

<file>|<subject_CN>|<signature_algo>|<notAfter>|<finding>

Where  
• <file> … just the base file name, e.g. `goodcorp.pem`  
• <subject_CN> … the CN value without the leading “CN=”  
• <signature_algo> … value taken verbatim from the “Signature Algorithm:” line  
• <notAfter> … value taken verbatim from the “Not After :” line  
• <finding> … vulnerability code determined as follows  
  - WEAK_SIG  → signature algorithm contains the substring **“md5”** or **“sha1”** (case-insensitive)  
  - EXPIRED   → the “Not After” date is earlier than the current system date (if both WEAK_SIG and EXPIRED apply, write **EXPIRED**)  
  - OK        → everything else  

Additional formatting rules:  
1. Order the lines alphabetically by the file name.  
2. End the log file with a single trailing newline (UNIX LF).  
3. Do **not** add any extra spaces before or after the pipe symbols.  

You may use whichever standard command-line tools you prefer (grep, awk, date, etc.).  
The final deliverable that the automated grader will check is the correctly formatted file  
/home/user/targets/scan_results/cert_vuln.log.
truth:
Pre-existing directory structure and file contents before the agent starts:

Directory: /home/user/targets/certs

File: /home/user/targets/certs/goodcorp.pem  
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
Subject: CN=good.example.com
Signature Algorithm: sha256WithRSAEncryption
Not After : Dec 31 23:59:59 2035 GMT
-----END CERTIFICATE-----

File: /home/user/targets/certs/weakcorp.pem  
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
Subject: CN=weak.example.com
Signature Algorithm: sha1WithRSAEncryption
Not After : Dec 31 23:59:59 2035 GMT
-----END CERTIFICATE-----

File: /home/user/targets/certs/oldcorp.pem  
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
Subject: CN=expired.example.com
Signature Algorithm: sha256WithRSAEncryption
Not After : Jan  1 00:00:00 2020 GMT
-----END CERTIFICATE-----

The directory /home/user/targets/scan_results **does not yet exist**.

Expected final artifact (to be created by the agent):

File: /home/user/targets/scan_results/cert_vuln.log  
Contents — exactly three lines followed by a trailing newline:

goodcorp.pem|good.example.com|sha256WithRSAEncryption|Dec 31 23:59:59 2035 GMT|OK
oldcorp.pem|expired.example.com|sha256WithRSAEncryption|Jan  1 00:00:00 2020 GMT|EXPIRED
weakcorp.pem|weak.example.com|sha1WithRSAEncryption|Dec 31 23:59:59 2035 GMT|WEAK_SIG
name: task_000000_92ef0995
